Patch Set 1:

> Hmm, I thought a similar patch from HPE was recently landed?
correct, but that patch sets the flag on ldiskfs's sb so that doesn't block _lustre_ umount.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Alex, could please explain when this is needed? Current code already used this flag at osd layer, and stack from umount is next (inside umount syscall)
[54467.503294] CPU: 5 PID: 49071 Comm: umount Kdump: loaded Tainted: P           OE    ---------r-  - 4.18.0-348.12.2.x6.1r.27.x86_64 #1

Patch Set 1:

> Alex, could please explain when this is needed? Current code already used this flag at osd layer, and stack from umount is next (inside umount syscall)

so with that flag set on ldiskfs's sb osd umount is blocked, but lustre's sb is not, so umount /mnt/lustre-ost1 can return immediately leaving actual lustre umount going in background.

Patch Set 1:

I agree with Alexander. With his patch, osd unmount happens on exit from the umount syscall, not in background.

Patch Set 1:

> I agree with Alexander. With his patch, osd unmount happens on exit from the umount syscall, not in background.

right, _osd_ umount does. lustre umount - does not, sometimes it goes async.

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > I agree with Alexander. With his patch, osd unmount happens on exit from the umount syscall, not in background.
> 
> right, _osd_ umount does. lustre umount - does not, sometimes it goes async.

I don't see any benefit from this patch still, and when it would be asynchronous.

Patch Set 1:

> I don't see any benefit from this patch still, and when it would be asynchronous.

the benefit is that HA scripts stopping Lustre doesn't need to use any kind of workaround/etc to just wait for umount completion. and then proceed with block device stuff, etc.
otherwise such a script can't deactive LVM group, for example as the device is still busy.

Patch Set 1:

I got your point about HA scripts etc. But could you please show asynchronous execution of umount, some cleanup_mnt() stack outside of umount process?

Patch Set 1:

So it should be easy to put dump_stack() at server_put_super() to show that it is called outside the umount thread?

Patch Set 1:

> So it should be easy to put dump_stack() at server_put_super() to show that it is called outside the umount thread?

not on my setup. but it was tested and proved to work on a production system.
though I'm fine to try reproduce this on a local system one more time.

parents.subject: LU-17198 tests: running_in_vm to recognize qemu

commit.subject: LU-17202 target: set SB_KERNMOUNT
commit.message:
LU-17202 target: set SB_KERNMOUNT

set SB_KERNMOUNT on server's mountpoint so umount process block
until the server (MDS/OST/MGS) is really umounted and released
the corresponding block device.

Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I03ac2dc6bb7cd4d93f5a1729fccb976aa1ebd5d4
